title: Entity Editor
text: Entity Editor The Entity Editor enables you to define an Entity and its Fields, Keys, and Relationships. Worksheets Define Structure —define the Entity's properties and fields, as well as its subtype entities (if any). Define Keys/Indexes —specify the fields that make up primary and candidate keys, and that will be indexed. Define Relationships —establish relationships between the Entity (and its subtypes) , and other entities. Relationships are owned by the Many entity. Write Script —define common or default behavior using ProcScript. How to Define a Modeled Entity Working with Development Objects Figure 1. Entity Editor showing Define Structure Worksheet Related Links Modeled Entities A Modeled Entity is a main development object that represents a logical data aggregation such as a database table,a collection of records, user interface controls, or other data structure. It is an abstract object that is intended for re-use in other objects, such as components, variables, and parameters. Entities An Entity defines a logical data aggregation. It usually represents a relational database table, but it is possible to define non-database entities for transient data, reusable user interface controls, or other application structures.
